---
title: "project2"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(gt)
library(reshape2)
library(WebPower)
library(MASS)
library(broom)
library(scales)


df <- read.csv("PrelimData.csv")

str(df)
dim(df)
head(df)


# Descriptive Statistics — TABLE 1
tbl1 <- df %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd}); [{min}, {max}]"),
    digits    = all_continuous() ~ 2,
    label = list(
      CVLT_CNG3 ~ "CVLT Change Score (1-year)",
      CORT_CNG3 ~ "Cortical Thickness Change (1-year, mm)",
      IL_6      ~ "Baseline IL-6 (pg/mL)",
      MCP_1     ~ "Baseline MCP-1 (pg/mL)"
    )
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 1. Descriptive Statistics (n = 30)**") %>%
  bold_labels()

tbl1


# Distribution Check + Outlier Detection
shapiro_vars <- map_df(names(df), function(v) {
  sw <- shapiro.test(df[[v]])
  tibble(Variable = v, W = round(sw$statistic, 4), p = round(sw$p.value, 4))
})
print(shapiro_vars)

# Outlier: > 3 SD from mean
outlier_check <- map_df(names(df), function(v) {
  flagged <- which(abs(scale(df[[v]])) > 3)
  tibble(Variable  = v,
         n_flagged = length(flagged),
         subjects  = if (length(flagged) > 0) paste(flagged, collapse = ", ")
                     else "none")
})
print(outlier_check)


# Correlation Analysis — FIGURE 1
# Rename for display
df_display <- df %>%
  rename(
    `CVLT\n(Cognitive)`    = CVLT_CNG3,
    `CORT\n(Cognitive)`    = CORT_CNG3,
    `IL-6\n(Inflammation)` = IL_6,
    `MCP-1\n(Inflammation)`= MCP_1
  )

cor_mat    <- cor(df_display, method = "pearson")
cor_melted <- melt(round(cor_mat, 2))

fig1 <- ggplot(cor_melted, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#4472C4",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#C0392B",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-1, 1),
    name     = "Correlation"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Figure 1. Pearson Correlation Matrix", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(fig1)

# Pairwise cor.test for text reporting
pairs <- list(
  c("IL_6",  "CVLT_CNG3"), c("IL_6",  "CORT_CNG3"),
  c("MCP_1", "CVLT_CNG3"), c("MCP_1", "CORT_CNG3"),
  c("IL_6",  "MCP_1"),     c("CVLT_CNG3", "CORT_CNG3")
)
cor_tests <- map_df(pairs, function(p) {
  ct <- cor.test(df[[p[1]]], df[[p[2]]])
  tibble(Var1 = p[1], Var2 = p[2],
         r    = round(ct$estimate, 3),
         CI_L = round(ct$conf.int[1], 3),
         CI_U = round(ct$conf.int[2], 3),
         p    = round(ct$p.value, 4))
})
print(cor_tests)


# Linear Regression — Aim 1a — TABLE 2
m1 <- lm(CVLT_CNG3 ~ IL_6,  data = df)
m2 <- lm(CORT_CNG3 ~ IL_6,  data = df)
m3 <- lm(CVLT_CNG3 ~ MCP_1, data = df)
m4 <- lm(CORT_CNG3 ~ MCP_1, data = df)

summary(m1); summary(m2); summary(m3); summary(m4)

tidy_model <- function(model, pred_name) {
  tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    rename(Beta = estimate, SE = std.error,
           CI_L = conf.low, CI_U = conf.high, p_value = p.value) %>%
    mutate(Predictor = pred_name,
           Outcome   = as.character(formula(model))[2],
           R2        = round(summary(model)$r.squared, 3)) %>%
    dplyr::select(Predictor, Outcome, Beta, SE, CI_L, CI_U, p_value, R2)
}

results <- bind_rows(
  tidy_model(m1, "IL-6"),  tidy_model(m2, "IL-6"),
  tidy_model(m3, "MCP-1"), tidy_model(m4, "MCP-1")
) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Beta, SE, CI_L, CI_U), ~ round(., 3)),
         p_value = round(p_value, 4))

print(results)

tbl2 <- results %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Table 2. Linear Regression Results - Aim 1a (n = 30)",
    subtitle = "Unadjusted models; outcome = 1-year change score"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    Predictor = "Predictor", Outcome = "Outcome",
    Beta = "β", SE = "SE",
    CI_L = "95% CI Lower", CI_U = "95% CI Upper",
    p_value = "p-value", R2 = "R²"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_body(columns = p_value, rows = p_value < 0.05)
  ) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    "Bold p-values: statistically significant at alpha = 0.05.
    Full models in the planned study will adjust for covariates including
    baseline outcome, age, sex, education, APOE, and vascular risk factors."
  )

tbl2


# Model Diagnostics (reported in Methods text, no figure)
models      <- list(m1, m2, m3, m4)
model_names <- c("IL-6->CVLT", "IL-6->CORT", "MCP-1->CVLT", "MCP-1->CORT")

resid_sw <- map_df(seq_along(models), function(i) {
  sw <- shapiro.test(residuals(models[[i]]))
  tibble(Model = model_names[i], W = round(sw$statistic, 4),
         p = round(sw$p.value, 4))
})
print(resid_sw)

cooks_res <- map_df(seq_along(models), function(i) {
  cd  <- cooks.distance(models[[i]])
  inf <- which(cd > 4 / nrow(df))
  tibble(Model   = model_names[i],
         flagged = if (length(inf) > 0) paste(inf, collapse = ", ") else "none")
})
print(cooks_res)


# Multiple Testing — Simulation
set.seed(2024)
n_sim          <- 100000
cor_predictors <- cor(df[, c("IL_6", "MCP_1")])
cor_outcomes   <- cor(df[, c("CVLT_CNG3", "CORT_CNG3")])
n              <- nrow(df)
min_pvals      <- numeric(n_sim)

for (sim in 1:n_sim) {
  X <- mvrnorm(n, mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = cor_predictors)
  Y <- mvrnorm(n, mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = cor_outcomes)
  p_vals <- c(
    summary(lm(Y[,1] ~ X[,1]))$coefficients[2, 4],
    summary(lm(Y[,2] ~ X[,1]))$coefficients[2, 4],
    summary(lm(Y[,1] ~ X[,2]))$coefficients[2, 4],
    summary(lm(Y[,2] ~ X[,2]))$coefficients[2, 4]
  )
  min_pvals[sim] <- min(p_vals)
}

adjusted_alpha   <- quantile(min_pvals, 0.05)
bonferroni_alpha <- 0.05 / 4
nominal_alpha    <- 0.05

print(tibble(
  Method = c("Nominal", "Simulation-based (FWER=0.05)", "Bonferroni"),
  Alpha  = round(c(nominal_alpha, adjusted_alpha, bonferroni_alpha), 4)
))


# Power Analysis — TABLE 3 + FIGURE 2 + FIGURE 3
n_planned <- 175
p1_aim1   <- 7;  p2_aim1 <- 6
p1_aim2   <- 10; p2_aim2 <- 9

r_obs <- c(
  "IL-6 -> CVLT"  = -0.2586,
  "IL-6 -> CORT"  = -0.5993,
  "MCP-1 -> CVLT" = -0.3184,
  "MCP-1 -> CORT" = -0.6853
)

# TABLE 3: Power summary

aim1_power <- map_df(names(r_obs), function(nm) {
  r  <- abs(r_obs[nm])
  f2 <- r^2 / (1 - r^2)
  pw_adj <- wp.regression(n=n_planned, p1=p1_aim1, p2=p2_aim1,
                           f2=f2, alpha=adjusted_alpha)
  pw_nom <- wp.regression(n=n_planned, p1=p1_aim1, p2=p2_aim1,
                           f2=f2, alpha=nominal_alpha)
  tibble(Aim         = "Aim 1a",
         Association = nm,
         r           = round(r_obs[nm], 3),
         f2          = round(f2, 4),
         Power_adj   = round(pw_adj$power, 3),
         Power_nom   = round(pw_nom$power, 3))
})

delta_r2_aim2 <- c(0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.08)
r2_main       <- 0.15

aim2_power <- map_df(delta_r2_aim2, function(dr2) {
  r2_full <- r2_main + dr2
  f2_incr <- dr2 / (1 - r2_full)
  pw_adj  <- wp.regression(n=n_planned, p1=p1_aim2, p2=p2_aim2,
                            f2=f2_incr, alpha=adjusted_alpha)
  pw_nom  <- wp.regression(n=n_planned, p1=p1_aim2, p2=p2_aim2,
                            f2=f2_incr, alpha=nominal_alpha)
  tibble(Aim         = "Aim 2",
         Association = paste0("Interaction (delta R2 = ", dr2, ")"),
         r           = NA_real_,
         f2          = round(f2_incr, 4),
         Power_adj   = round(pw_adj$power, 3),
         Power_nom   = round(pw_nom$power, 3))
})

power_all <- bind_rows(aim1_power, aim2_power)
print(power_all)

tbl3 <- power_all %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Table 3. Estimated Statistical Power (n = 175)",
    subtitle = paste0("Sim-adjusted alpha = ", round(adjusted_alpha, 3),
                      "; nominal alpha = 0.05")
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    Aim         = "Aim",
    Association = "Association / Effect",
    r           = "Bivariate r",
    f2          = "Cohen's f2",
    Power_adj   = paste0("Power (sim-adj a=",
                         round(adjusted_alpha, 3), ")"),
    Power_nom   = "Power (a=0.05)"
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Power_adj, Power_nom),
                           rows    = Power_adj >= 0.80)
  ) %>%
  tab_row_group(label = "Aim 2: Interaction Effect",
                rows  = Aim == "Aim 2") %>%
  tab_row_group(label = "Aim 1a: Main Effect",
                rows  = Aim == "Aim 1a") %>%
  tab_footnote(
    "Bold = Power >= 0.80. Aim 1a: p1=7, p2=6 (1 marker + 6 covariates).
    Aim 2: p1=10, p2=9 (SUVR + marker + interaction + baseline + 6 covariates).
    Bivariate r from preliminary data (n=30) used as illustrative effect size
    approximations; partial effects in adjusted models may differ.
    Aim 1b and full marker set (IL-6, TNF-alpha, MCP-1, Eotaxin-1) will be
    evaluated in the complete analytic dataset."
  ) %>%
  fmt_missing(columns = "r", missing_text = "-")

tbl3

# ── FIGURE 2: Power vs r (clean, no grey vertical lines) ─────────────────────

r_range <- seq(0.10, 0.75, by = 0.005)

power_curve <- map_df(r_range, function(r) {
  f2 <- r^2 / (1 - r^2)
  pw <- wp.regression(n=n_planned, p1=p1_aim1, p2=p2_aim1,
                      f2=f2, alpha=adjusted_alpha)
  tibble(r = r, power = pw$power)
})

min_r80 <- power_curve %>% filter(power >= 0.80) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(r)

fig2 <- ggplot(power_curve, aes(x = r, y = power)) +
  geom_line(color = "#4472C4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, color = "red",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = min_r80, color = "darkgreen",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("text", x = min_r80 + 0.02, y = 0.45,
           label = paste0("r \u2248 ", round(min_r80, 2)),
           color = "darkgreen", size = 4, hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.65, y = 0.83,
           label = "80% Power", color = "red", size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.1, 0.7, 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25), labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title    = "Figure 2. Power vs Effect Size for Aim 1a (n = 175)",
    subtitle = paste0("Covariate-adjusted model (p1=7, p2=6); ",
                      "sim-adjusted alpha = ", round(adjusted_alpha, 3)),
    x = "Effect size (r; bivariate estimates from preliminary data)",
    y = "Statistical Power"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"))

print(fig2)

# FIGURE 3: Required sample size vs r

r_for_n <- seq(0.10, 0.75, by = 0.005)

n_required <- map_df(r_for_n, function(r) {
  f2 <- r^2 / (1 - r^2)
  lo <- 10; hi <- 2000
  while (hi - lo > 1) {
    mid <- floor((lo + hi) / 2)
    pw  <- wp.regression(n=mid, p1=p1_aim1, p2=p2_aim1,
                         f2=f2, alpha=adjusted_alpha)
    if (pw$power >= 0.80) hi <- mid else lo <- mid
  }
  tibble(r = r, n_required = hi)
})

fig3 <- ggplot(n_required, aes(x = r, y = n_required)) +
  geom_line(color = "#4472C4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = n_planned, color = "red",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = min_r80, color = "darkgreen",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("text", x = min_r80 + 0.02, y = 700,
           label = paste0("r \u2248 ", round(min_r80, 2)),
           color = "darkgreen", size = 4, hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.55, y = n_planned + 45,
           label = paste0("n = ", n_planned, " (planned)"),
           color = "red", size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.1, 0.7, 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1000), breaks = seq(0, 1000, 200)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Figure 3. Required Sample Size vs Effect Size for Aim 1a",
    subtitle = paste0("Target power = 80%; covariate-adjusted model (p1=7, p2=6); ",
                      "sim-adjusted alpha = ", round(adjusted_alpha, 3)),
    x = "Effect size (r; bivariate estimates from preliminary data)",
    y = "Required Sample Size"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"))

print(fig3)
```
